name: Sorbet Au Sauterne (Sauterne Sherbet)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the gelatin and 1/2 cup sugar in a 2-quart saucepan.
2. Stir in 1 cup water and the salt. Heat to boiling, stirring constantly.
3. Continue boiling for 5 minutes. Remove from heat.
4. Stir in wine and lemon juice. Cool to room temperature.
5. Pour into an ice cube tray. Freeze until mushy and partially frozen (about 1 hour 20 minutes).
6. In a 1-1/2 quart saucepan, heat the sugar, water, and cream of tartar over medium heat until boiling.
7. Boil without stirring to 240°F on a candy thermometer.
8. Beat egg whites in a small bowl until soft peaks form.
9. Continue beating while pouring the hot syrup into the egg whites in thin streams.
10. Beat until the mixture is cool (about 8 minutes).
11. Spoon the partially frozen mixture into a chilled large mixing bowl.
12. Beat with a rotary beater until fluffy; fold in the egg white mixture.
13. Pour into an ungreased baking pan, 9x9x2 inches.
14. Freeze until firm (about 3 hours).
15. Serve in chilled sherbet glasses.
